Oracc: CAMS/GKAB — CAMS Geography of Knowledge Corpus

SpTU 2, 017 [House building rituals][via cams/gkab]

Obverse
Column i
o io i  (6 lines missing)
o i 1'1'

[...] x

o i 2'2'

[...] x

o i 3'3'

[...] ⸢A⸣ LI E

o i 4'4'

[...] x ⸢GIŠ?⸣ x gišGEŠTIN.UD.A ZI₃ zi₃EŠA

(o i 4') You strew [...] ..., raisins, flour, fine flour, [...].

o i 5'5'

[...] tu-sar-raq

o i 6'6'

2 ḪAR KU₃.BABBAR 2 ḪAR KU₃.SI₂₂ kušE.SIR₂ kušDU₈.ŠI.A

(o i 6') You set in place 2 rings (made) of silver, 2 rings (made) of gold, a shoe (made) of untanned leather, 7 flasks (full) of good oil, 7 flasks (full) of cedar oil, 7 flasks (full) of honey, 7 pots (full) of butter, [...] ... [...].

o i 7'7'

7 dugŠAGAN I₃ DU₁₀.GA 7 dugŠAGAN I₃ gišEREN

o i 8'8'

⸢7 dugŠAGAN LAL₃⸣ 7 ⸢DUG⸣ I₃.NUN.NA

o i 9'9'

[...] x x x

o i 10'10'

[...] ⸢GAR⸣an

(rest of column missing)
Column ii
o ii 1o ii 1

[...] ⸢ana⸣ dNIN.GA.RAŠ KU₃.GA u dARA tara-kas₂

(o ii 1) You set out [...] for pure Ningaraš and Usmu.

o ii 22

[x x]-⸢MEŠ⸣ ina ŠA₃ ŠUTUG ana DINGIR E₂ dINANA E₂ u dLAMMA E₂!(UŠ) tara-kas₂

(o ii 2) You set out [...] inside a reed-hut for the god of the house, the goddess of the house and the protective spirit of the house.

o ii 33

[x x] ⸢ana⸣ IGI E₂ tara-kas₂

(o ii 3) You set out [...] in front of the house.

o ii 44

[x x] TA UR₃ ur-ra-dam-ma

(o ii 4) He (Kulla) [...] comes down from the roof and you seat him [...] front of the ritual assemblages of Ea and Marduk and you set out a ritual assemblage in front of him.

o ii 55

[x] ⸢IGI⸣ KEŠ₂ ša₂ dIDIM u dAMAR.UTU tu-še-<še>-eb-šu₂-ma

o ii 66

KEŠ₂ ana IGI-šu₂ tara-kas₂ i-si-iḫ-tu₄

(o ii 6) Before Šamaš you disturb an allocation (of offerings) thus on the roof in front of him.

o ii 77

ki-a ina? UR₃ ina IGI dUTU ina IGI-šu₂ tu-se-eḫ-ḫe

o ii 88

ina pa-ni-šu₂ tu-us-saḫ TA KEŠ₂ tu₄-uš-te-li-mu

(o ii 8) You allocate (it) in front of him.

(o ii 8) After you have completed the ritual assemblage, the mašmaššu-priest stands in front of Ea and Marduk and recites 'Ea and Marduk, lords of heaven (and) earth' 3 times.

o ii 99

lu₂MAŠ.MAŠ ana IGI dIDIM u dAMAR.UTU GUBaz-ma

o ii 1010

de₂-a u dAMAR.UTU EN-MEŠ ANe KIti₃ 3-šu₂ ŠIDnu

o ii 1111

GIM ŠIDu₂ KEŠ₂ DU₃.A.BI DU₈-ma

(o ii 11) When he has recited (it), you clear away all the ritual assemblages and the builder raises Kulla and he removes Kulla's allocation, head (and) nose, as much as there is, and the mašmaššu-priest raises a spade (made) of copper and scrapes the dust off the altar-base of Kulla on the roof of the house and off the door-jambs.

o ii 1212

ŠITIM? dSIG₄ i-na-aš₂-<ši>-ma

o ii 1313

SAG.DU ap-pi i-si-iḫ-tu₄ dSIG₄ ma-la ba-šu-u₂

o ii 1414

i-na-aš₂-ši-ma lu₂MAŠ.MAŠ gišMAR URUDU IL₂-ma

o ii 1515

SAḪAR-ḪI.A šu-bat dSIG₄ ša₂ UR₃ E₂ u ZA₃.DU₈-MEŠ i-ḫal-la-aš₂

o ii 1616

ina giDUSU U₄-1-KAM is-si-ip KI i-si-iḫ-tu₄

(o ii 16) He collects (it) in an earth-carrying basket for a day. He brings (it) out with that allocation and [...] ... at the outer door he recites a recitation behind Kulla.

o ii 1717

MU-MEŠ uš-te-eṣ-ṣe-ma

o ii 1818

[x] x A KI KA₂ AN.AŠ.AM₃ EGIR dSIG₄ ŠIDtu₂ ŠIDnu

o ii 1919

[...] ⸢BI?⸣ ID₂ qa₂-du qi₂-da!(ŠA-)ti ŠU x

(o ii 19) [...] ... the watercourse as far as downstream ... he goes [...] his ....

o ii 2020

[...] x-šu₂ il-lak

o ii 2121

[...] x x [x] x

(o ii 21) [...] ... [...] ...

(rest of column missing)
Reverse
Column i
(start of column missing)
r i 1'r 1'

[...] x

(r i 1') You heap up [...] ... [...] ..., lentils, [...] ... [...].

r i 2'2'

[...] x x GU₂.TUR

r i 3'3'

[...] x [x x] DUBak

r i 4'4'

[... GAR]an GIM ser₂-qu tu-⸢sar⸣-ra-qu

(r i 4') You set in place [...]. When you strew the offerings, you drip cedar [...] on top.

r i 5'5'

[x giš]⸢EREN⸣ ina UGU tu-na-tak

r i 6'6'

ana IGI ⸢7⸣ giMA.SA₂.AB 7 UDU.NITA₂ BALqi₂

(r i 6') You present 7 sheep in front of 7 baskets.

r i 7'7'

uzuME.ḪE₂ u uzuKA.IZI GARan

(r i 7') You set in place fatty tissue and roast meat.

r i 8'8'

7 KEŠ₂ tara-kas₂ 14 dugA.DA.GUR₅ GUBan

(r i 8') You set out 7 ritual assemblages. You set up 14 libation vessels.

r i 9'9'

i-na UR₃ E₂ BI 7 gišGU.ZA-MEŠ ŠUBdi

(r i 9') You lay down 7 thrones on the roof of that house.

r i 10'10'

7 TUG₂ BABBAR ina i-si-iḫ-ti-šu₂-nu 7 tug₂GADA ina i-si-iḫ-ti-šu₂-nu

(r i 10') 7 white garments (are) in their allocation, 7 linen garments (are) in their allocation.

r i 11'11'

ṣer-pi₂ DU₃.A.BI ina UGU GAR.GAR 7 GI.DU₈ ana IGI KEŠ₂

(r i 11') You continue to put all the red wool on top. You set out 7 offering tables in front.

r i 12'12'

7-TA.AM₃ NINDA IMGAGA ina muḫ-ḫi GAR.GAR

(r i 12') For each of the 7 you continue to put a loaf (made) of emmer on top.

r i 13'13'

NIG₂.NA ŠIM-ḪI.A GAR.GARan 7 dugNIG₂.DUR₂.BUR₃-MEŠ GUBan

(r i 13') You continue to set in place a censer (full) of aromatics. You set up 7 tubs.

r i 14'14'

ab-ri ša₂ GI DU₁₀.GA ina muḫ-ḫi 4 SIG₄ ta-nap-<pa>-ḫa

(r i 14') You light a pile of sweet reeds on top of 4 bricks.

r i 15'15'

uduSILA₄ SA₅ UŠ₂ gišEREN GU₂-su TAG-ma KU₅is

(r i 15') A red lamb -- you apply cedar resin to its neck and cut (its neck).

r i 16'16'

TUG₂ BABBAR MU₄.MU₄-ma? ta-qal-lu

(r i 16') You put on a white garment and you roast (the lamb).

r i 17'17'

⸢7 dugNIG₂⸣.DUR₂.BUR₃-MEŠ tu-maš-šar-ma tuš-ken

(r i 17') You leave the 7 tubs and you prostrate yourself.

r i 18'18'

[...] x 3 ⸢gi⸣MA.SA₂.AB GUBan 12-TA.AM₃

(r i 18') You set up [...] ... 3 baskets. For each of the 12
